regimeSub: Iran-war energy shock pushes CPI above 4% while a Warsh-led Fed kills cut bias and threatens a hike into slowing 1.6% growth.
themes: Hormuz tankers trapped -> energy-led CPI to 4.2% -> Fed pivots from cuts to a possible September hike.
US-Iran peace talks postponed -> Brent stuck near $80 with a war premium -> sticky inflation, no Fed relief.
Warsh Fed pares cutting bias -> real rates grind higher -> equities at record 7500 priced for perfection look fragile.
Stagflation mix (4.2% CPI, 1.6% GDP) -> gold rips to $4224 as the cleanest hedge against policy error and war.

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy real rate negative (-0.6%); 10y real ~+0.3%. Fed behind the curve, hike talk = real rates push up.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +29bp, modestly positive. Bear-steepening risk if war premium entrenches inflation and Fed signals hike.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Still tight despite stagflation; complacent. First crack if oil re-spikes or Fed hikes into slowdown.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.4 — far too cheap given trapped tankers, CPI risk and hawkish Fed. Buy convexity into the prints.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $79.85, holding war premium even as peace talks slip. Hormuz risk is the dominant inflation swing factor.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4224 record — the regime trade. Bid on war, negative real rates and Fed credibility doubt under Warsh.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1538 — won weak on oil-import drag and risk-off. Asian energy importers most exposed to Brent premium.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Micron 6/24 print is the tell; AI demand intact but rate/oil shock caps multiples. Watch for air pocket.

headline: An Iran-war oil shock has driven US inflation back above 4%, a newly hawkish Warsh Fed is threatening to hike into 1.6% growth, and record equities and a 16 VIX are dangerously underpricing stagflation.
